actor_claims.quote_text: Thoma Bravo’s outside counsel, Kirkland & Ellis LLP (“ Kirkland ”)

actor_claims.quote_text: two sources of potential debt financing

actor_relation_claims.object_label: the Special Committee and the Board
actor_relation_claims.quote_text: the advice Barclays would provide to the Special Committee and the Board
based in part on the advice of Goodwin
Thoma Bravo’s outside counsel, Kirkland & Ellis LLP (“ Kirkland ”) sent a revised draft of the merger agreement to Goodwin.

the Company permitted two sources of potential debt financing, each of which Barclays identified as parties that could potentially provide debt financing to Thoma Bravo, to join as parties under the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.
actor_relation_claims.subject_label: two sources of potential debt financing

bid_claims.quote_text: Thoma Bravo’s bid was at a price of $19.00 per share in cash, provided for an equity commitment for the entire purchase price and indicated that Thoma Bravo had completed all of its due diligence and was prepared to execute its revised draft of the merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to the market open on July 11, 2016.

Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.

event_claims.description: The Board authorized Barclays to advance Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo to the second phase of the strategic process and encourage higher offer prices.
event_claims.quote_text: the Board authorized Barclays to advance all three parties to the second phase of the strategic process and to encourage them to indicate a willingness to increase their respective offer prices.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A was contacted and told it would need to materially increase its offer price to continue in the strategic process.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 13, 2016, representatives of Barclays contacted Sponsor A and indicated that its June 9 bid was on the low end and that in order for Sponsor A to continue in the strategic process, it would need to materially increase its offer price.

event_claims.description: Strategic 3 informed Barclays it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 14, 2016, Strategic 3 informed representatives of Barclays that because of its internal focus on other corporate transactions and a perceived overlap in technologies, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A did not continue discussions after indicating any second round bid would not be meaningfully higher than its June 9 indication.
event_claims.quote_text: Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor A and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

event_claims.description: The Board established a Special Committee consisting only of independent and disinterested directors.
event_claims.quote_text: Following this discussion, the Board established a special committee of the Board (the “ Special Committee ”) that included only independent and disinterested directors.

event_claims.description: The Special Committee directed Barclays to contact Strategic 4 to determine whether it might have interest in submitting an indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: The Special Committee directed Barclays to contact an additional strategic party, an enterprise software company (“ Strategic 4 ”), to determine whether it might have interest in submitting an indication of interest.

event_claims.description: Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo were provided access to an online data room containing nonpublic information regarding the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 17, 2016, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo were provided access to an online data room containing nonpublic information regarding the Company.

event_claims.description: Strategic 4 informed Barclays that it was not interested in participating in the strategic process.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 23, 2016, Strategic 4 informed Barclays that an acquisition of the Company would not be a strategic fit for it and that it was not interested in participating in the strategic process.

event_claims.description: Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: Later on June 24, 2016, Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company’s proposed form of merger agreement (which would be provided in due course) by July 7, 2016, and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.

event_claims.description: Sponsor B did not continue discussions after indicating any final bid would be significantly below its June 9 indication.
event_claims.quote_text: Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor B and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

event_claims.actor_label: two sources of potential debt financing
event_claims.description: Two potential debt financing sources were permitted to join the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 5 and 6, 2016, in response to Thoma Bravo’s request to work with potential debt financing sources, the Company permitted two sources of potential debt financing, each of which Barclays identified as parties that could potentially provide debt financing to Thoma Bravo, to join as parties under the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.

event_claims.description: On the final bid date, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 8, 2016, the date by which Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had been invited to submit their final bids, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company.

event_claims.description: Thoma Bravo's bid provided for an equity commitment for the entire purchase price.
event_claims.quote_text: Thoma Bravo’s bid was at a price of $19.00 per share in cash, provided for an equity commitment for the entire purchase price and indicated that Thoma Bravo had completed all of its due diligence and was prepared to execute its revised draft of the merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to the market open on July 11, 2016.

event_claims.description: Barclays received Thoma Bravo's revised written best and final offer of $19.25 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Later on July 9, 2016, Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.

event_claims.description: Thoma Bravo submitted a revised draft equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: because Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had submitted proposals within close range of each other
because Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had submitted proposals within close range of each other
